TJX Companies Q3 2025 Earnings Call TranscriptProvided by QuartrNovember 20, 2024 ShareLink copied to clipboard.Key Takeaways
Q3 comp store sales rose 3% (high end of plan), all divisions delivered growth via customer transactions—TJX International comps +7%—while pre-tax profit margin reached 12.3% (+30 bps) and EPS was $1.14 (+11%), prompting full-year outlook raises.
Full-year guidance was raised: same-store sales +3%, consolidated sales of $55.9–56.1 B, pre-tax profit margin of 11.3% (+40 bps vs. prior year), and EPS of $4.15–4.17 (+10–11%).
Q4 guidance anticipates comp growth of 2–3%, sales of $15.9–16.1 B, pre-tax margin of 10.8–10.9% (down 10 bps), and EPS of $1.12–1.14 amid modest headwinds from shrink accrual and net interest income.
HomeGoods celebrated its 1,000th store, achieved a 3% comp increase and expanded segment profit margin by 200 bps, benefiting from stronger merchandise margin and fewer e-commerce closure impacts.
Long-term growth initiatives include opening 1,200+ new stores in existing markets, launching TK Maxx in Spain in early 2026, and expanding off-price JVs in Mexico, UAE, and Saudi Arabia to capture global off-price demand.

About TJX CompaniesTJX Companies (NYSE:TJX), Inc. is a leading off-price retailer of apparel, footwear, home fashions and other consumer goods. The company operates multiple retail concepts that offer discounted brand-name and designer merchandise, including well-known banners such as T.J. Maxx and Marshalls in the United States, HomeGoods for home furnishings, TK Maxx in parts of Europe, and Winners and Homesense in Canada. Merchandise categories span women's, men's and children’s apparel, accessories, beauty, home décor, kitchenware and small furniture, with frequent changes in assortment that create a “treasure-hunt” shopping experience for consumers.
The company’s business model centers on opportunistic buying, purchasing excess, irregular or out-of-season inventory from manufacturers, department stores and other suppliers, and passing savings to customers through lower prices. TJX operates a predominantly brick-and-mortar footprint supported by distribution and logistics networks, alongside growing digital capabilities and e-commerce channels to complement in-store sales. Its retail strategy emphasizes high inventory turnover, broad assortment variety, and localized merchandise mixes to meet regional consumer preferences.
TJX serves consumers across the United States, Canada and parts of Europe and has expanded its store base and geographic reach through ongoing new-store openings and selective international growth. The company is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol TJX and is headquartered in Framingham, Massachusetts. Over time it has become one of the largest off-price retailers globally, operating under a centralized corporate structure with regional management overseeing store operations, sourcing and distribution.View

TJX Companies Q4 2025 Earnings Call TranscriptProvided by QuartrFebruary 26, 2025 ShareLink copied to clipboard.Key Takeaways
TJX delivered Q4 comp sales growth of 5%, with full-year sales of $56.4 B and EPS up 13% to $4.26, driven by broad-based transaction increases across all divisions including Canada (+10%) and International (+7%).
Gross margin expanded by 100 bps in Q4 to 30.6% for the year and pre-tax profit margin rose to 11.5%, fueled by lower shrink, strong mark-on and operating leverage on above-plan sales.
For fiscal 2026, the company forecasts 2%–3% comp store sales growth, consolidated sales of $58.1–$58.6 B and EPS of $4.34–$4.43, with slight margin headwinds from foreign exchange and tariffs.
TJX plans to open 130 net new stores in FY26 across its global banners, invest $2.1–$2.2 B in capital expenditures, and returned $4.1 B to shareholders in FY25 while ending with $5.3 B in cash.
The company highlights its value leadership, flexible buying model, treasure-hunt shopping experience and a long-term store potential of 7,000 locations, complemented by new joint ventures in Mexico and the Middle East.

About TJX CompaniesTJX Companies (NYSE:TJX), Inc. is a leading off-price retailer of apparel, footwear, home fashions and other consumer goods. The company operates multiple retail concepts that offer discounted brand-name and designer merchandise, including well-known banners such as T.J. Maxx and Marshalls in the United States, HomeGoods for home furnishings, TK Maxx in parts of Europe, and Winners and Homesense in Canada. Merchandise categories span women's, men's and children’s apparel, accessories, beauty, home décor, kitchenware and small furniture, with frequent changes in assortment that create a “treasure-hunt” shopping experience for consumers.
The company’s business model centers on opportunistic buying, purchasing excess, irregular or out-of-season inventory from manufacturers, department stores and other suppliers, and passing savings to customers through lower prices. TJX operates a predominantly brick-and-mortar footprint supported by distribution and logistics networks, alongside growing digital capabilities and e-commerce channels to complement in-store sales. Its retail strategy emphasizes high inventory turnover, broad assortment variety, and localized merchandise mixes to meet regional consumer preferences.
TJX serves consumers across the United States, Canada and parts of Europe and has expanded its store base and geographic reach through ongoing new-store openings and selective international growth. The company is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol TJX and is headquartered in Framingham, Massachusetts. Over time it has become one of the largest off-price retailers globally, operating under a centralized corporate structure with regional management overseeing store operations, sourcing and distribution.View
